title: Volumes, PersistentVolumes, Claims, StorageClasses, CSI, Snapshots, and Recovery · Guided Manifest Studio
summary: A platform pairing session predicts each API transition before producing the first working stateful storage and recovery contract for volumes, persistentvolumes, claims, storageclasses, csi, snapshots, and recovery. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of stateful storage and recovery contract while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish image, writable layer, emptyDir, projected, hostPath, ephemeral CSI, persistent, and external application storage lifecycles and authority.
Trace PV and PVC capacity, access modes, volume mode, selectors, binding, claim references, node affinity, phases, expansion, and release.
Design StorageClasses with provisioner, parameters, defaulting, reclaim policy, expansion, mount options, and WaitForFirstConsumer topology behavior.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Volume Lifecycle Types
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Distinguish image, writable layer, emptyDir, projected, hostPath, ephemeral CSI, persistent, and external application storage lifecycles and authority.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Volume Lifecycle Types.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish image, writable layer, emptydir, projected, hostpath, ephemeral csi, persistent, and external application storage lifecycles and authority. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop K8s Volume Lifecycle Types, keep K8s Volume Lifecycle Types connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Append the Kubernetes evidence item headed "  # Evidence: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore-bkhowu". Tailor its storage-recovery.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic API or manifest review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run kubectl, Helm, kubeadm, and cluster commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns K8s App Consistent Backup Restore into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore-bkhowu
  - competency: k8s-app-consistent-backup-restore
    artifact: storage-recovery.yaml
    decision: app consistent backup restore
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: rendered manifests immutable digest policy and rollout status
    case-key: bkhowu
    change: |
      claim: intake-data
      storageClass: retained-zonal
      volumeBindingMode: WaitForFirstConsumer
      reclaimPolicy: Retain
      snapshot: application-quiesced
      restore: fresh-namespace-and-claim
      checksum: verified
    verify: kubectl get pv,pvc,storageclass,volumesnapshot -A -o json
    failure: Reject Bound as durability proof, Delete reclaim surprises, arbitrary-zone binding, snapshot-as-backup claims, or restore without application validation.
    repair: Trace claim and volume lifecycle, align topology and access, quiesce the application, protect metadata and keys, and rehearse clean restore.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

At Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, trace this concrete input and predict admission, defaulting, scheduling, reconciliation, status, traffic, storage, policy, disruption, and cleanup evidence before reviewing it: A workshop StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

For Test an acceptance boundary · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, challenge the documented misconception with object, condition, event, policy, failure, repair, rollback, and transfer evidence: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.

stimulus.title: K8s App Consistent Backup Restore failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A Bound PVC and successful VolumeSnapshot prove the application can recover within its objectives.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated K8s App Consistent Backup Restore condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

For test an acceptance boundary, copy one familiar manifest or kubectl command, omit context, API discovery, desired-versus-observed status, failure, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer checks, then accept an Applied response as production proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore
steps.instruction:
Write the workshop evidence note for K8s App Consistent Backup Restore: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A workshop StatefulSet loses a node while its PVC is bound in another zone, reclaim policy is Delete, expansion is pending, and the backup has never restored. Defend StorageClass, binding, access mode, topology, snapshot, restore, and application consistency. Competency probe: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Workshop K8s App Consistent Backup Restore, keep K8s App Consistent Backup Restore connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Define application quiescence, snapshot or logical backup, metadata, encryption, checksums, retention, cross-cluster restore, and changed-case validation.
